source_note: Expanded from data/validation/historical_cases.json. Evidence content was not retrieved under WO-010; original publisher URLs are retained for independent verification.

events.title: Crude and refined product prices rise sharply, raising bunker and domestic fuel costs
thailand_relevance_basis: Thailand imports refined product and prices domestic diesel against international benchmarks, so an international energy shock transmits into domestic transport cost.
Bunker fuel is a direct input to ocean freight cost on every Thailand lane.

transmission_chain.external_driver: A sharp rise in international crude and refined product prices.
transmission_chain.operational_change: Bunker fuel and domestic diesel prices rose alongside the international benchmark.
transmission_chain.logistics_mechanism: Bunker cost is a direct input to ocean freight pricing and is commonly recovered through bunker surcharges; domestic diesel is a direct input to inland drayage cost.
transmission_chain.observable_indicator: Published crude benchmarks and Thailand retail diesel prices.
transmission_chain.outcome: Potential cost pressure across Thailand ocean lanes and the domestic port connection, with no measured effect on any Thailand shipment price.

known_limitations: Not assessed for this area at the case cutoff; this is a gap, not a finding of no impact.
Not assessed for this area at the case cutoff; this is a gap, not a finding of no impact.

transmission_mechanism: Sustained high bunker cost can prompt slow steaming, which lengthens transit time.
known_limitations: Whether carriers actually adjusted service speed is not established by any qualified source.
Not assessed for this area at the case cutoff; this is a gap, not a finding of no impact.
Not assessed for this area at the case cutoff; this is a gap, not a finding of no impact.

transmission_mechanism: Higher bunker prices raise the fuel component of ocean freight cost and are commonly recovered through bunker surcharges.
Higher domestic diesel prices raise inland drayage cost between Thailand ports and the hinterland.
known_limitations: Pass-through from a crude benchmark to a bunker price, and from a bunker price to a freight rate, is neither immediate nor proportional. No Thailand freight rate source is qualified, so no cost effect is quantified.
Not assessed for this area at the case cutoff; this is a gap, not a finding of no impact.
Not assessed for this area at the case cutoff; this is a gap, not a finding of no impact.
Not assessed for this area at the case cutoff; this is a gap, not a finding of no impact.
Historical validation case HVC-005, assessed at cutoff 2022-06-30T00:00:00Z. Later knowledge is deliberately excluded.

The eventual peak and duration of the price movement were unknown at this cutoff. This case exists to check that an external driver with a complete chain produces potential cost pressure rather than a quantified cost figure.

clustering.title_normalized: crude and refined product prices rise sharply, raising bunker and domestic fuel costs

events.title: Panama Canal Authority restricts daily transit slots during drought conditions
thailand_relevance_basis: The Panama Canal is a routing option for Asia to United States East and Gulf Coast services, which is one Thailand lane.

Whether any given Thailand service actually routes via Panama rather than Suez is carrier-specific and is not published by any source qualified in this registry, so exposure is potential rather than confirmed.

transmission_chain.external_driver: Prolonged drought reduced the water available to operate the canal's lock system.
transmission_chain.operational_change: The canal authority reduced the number of daily transit slots and applied draft restrictions.
transmission_chain.logistics_mechanism: Fewer slots force waiting, slot auctions, or rerouting via Suez or the United States West Coast, each of which changes transit time or cost.
transmission_chain.observable_indicator: Canal authority advisories stating the slot count and draft limit.
transmission_chain.outcome: Potential transit-time and cost effects on Thailand services routed to the United States East and Gulf Coast via Panama.

known_limitations: Not assessed for this area at the case cutoff; this is a gap, not a finding of no impact.
Not assessed for this area at the case cutoff; this is a gap, not a finding of no impact.

transmission_mechanism: Reduced daily slots force waiting or rerouting for services that transit the canal.
known_limitations: Which Thailand services transit Panama is not published by any qualified source, so exposure is unconfirmed.
Not assessed for this area at the case cutoff; this is a gap, not a finding of no impact.
Not assessed for this area at the case cutoff; this is a gap, not a finding of no impact.

transmission_mechanism: Slot scarcity raises the cost of securing a transit, whether through auction premiums or a longer alternative routing.
known_limitations: No Thailand-origin freight rate source is qualified; no Thailand cost effect is quantified.
Not assessed for this area at the case cutoff; this is a gap, not a finding of no impact.
Not assessed for this area at the case cutoff; this is a gap, not a finding of no impact.
Not assessed for this area at the case cutoff; this is a gap, not a finding of no impact.
Historical validation case HVC-002, assessed at cutoff 2023-11-15T00:00:00Z. Later knowledge is deliberately excluded.
How long the restrictions lasted, and whether slot counts were later restored, was unknown at this cutoff.

events.title: Container carriers divert Asia–Europe services away from the Red Sea and Suez routing

lane_relevance.basis: lane includes country EG; lane transits chokepoint CHK-BABELMANDEB; lane transits chokepoint CHK-SUEZ
lane transits chokepoint CHK-BABELMANDEB; lane transits chokepoint CHK-SUEZ

thailand_relevance_basis: Thailand westbound container services to Northern Europe and the Mediterranean normally transit Bab el-Mandeb and the Suez Canal, so a routing change on that corridor lengthens the Thailand lanes that use it.
The relationship is a lane-transit relationship, not a Thailand-specific event: nothing here establishes an effect on any particular Thailand service or shipper.

transmission_chain.external_driver: Security risk to merchant shipping in the southern Red Sea and Gulf of Aden.
transmission_chain.operational_change: Carriers rerouted Asia–Europe services around the Cape of Good Hope instead of transiting Bab el-Mandeb and Suez.
transmission_chain.logistics_mechanism: A longer routing absorbs more vessel days per round trip, which withdraws effective capacity from the service string and shifts arrival schedules.
transmission_chain.observable_indicator: Published carrier service advisories, Suez Canal transit counts, and container freight benchmarks for Asia–Europe routings.
transmission_chain.outcome: Observed increase in transit time on affected Asia–Europe strings, with potential cost and capacity effects on Thailand lanes routed through the same corridor.

impact_assessments.geographic_scope: Bab el-Mandeb Strait, Suez Canal, Middle East and Gulf
known_limitations: Not assessed for this area at the case cutoff; this is a gap, not a finding of no impact.

impact_assessments.geographic_scope: Bab el-Mandeb Strait, Suez Canal, Middle East and Gulf
known_limitations: Not assessed for this area at the case cutoff; this is a gap, not a finding of no impact.

impact_assessments.geographic_scope: Bab el-Mandeb Strait, Suez Canal, Middle East and Gulf
transmission_mechanism: Diversion around the Cape of Good Hope lengthens the routing and adds vessel days per round trip.
known_limitations: Transit-time effect is established for the corridor, not measured for any individual Thailand service.

impact_assessments.geographic_scope: Bab el-Mandeb Strait, Suez Canal, Middle East and Gulf
transmission_mechanism: Longer transit on Thailand westbound lanes shifts delivery timing for goods moving to European markets.
known_limitations: No Thailand trade series was retrieved to confirm a flow effect.

impact_assessments.geographic_scope: Bab el-Mandeb Strait, Suez Canal, Middle East and Gulf
known_limitations: Not assessed for this area at the case cutoff; this is a gap, not a finding of no impact.

impact_assessments.geographic_scope: Bab el-Mandeb Strait, Suez Canal, Middle East and Gulf
transmission_mechanism: Reduced effective capacity and higher fuel consumption per voyage exert upward pressure on freight benchmarks for affected routings.
known_limitations: No Thailand-origin freight rate source is qualified, so no Thailand cost effect is quantified anywhere.

impact_assessments.geographic_scope: Bab el-Mandeb Strait, Suez Canal, Middle East and Gulf
transmission_mechanism: Absorbing additional vessel days on the same fleet withdraws effective capacity from the trade.
known_limitations: Capacity withdrawal is inferred from routing length; no deployed-capacity series is qualified in this registry.

impact_assessments.geographic_scope: Bab el-Mandeb Strait, Suez Canal, Middle East and Gulf
transmission_mechanism: Longer round trips shift published arrival schedules and degrade schedule reliability on affected strings.
known_limitations: Schedule reliability is not measured by any source qualified in this registry.

impact_assessments.geographic_scope: Bab el-Mandeb Strait, Suez Canal, Middle East and Gulf
known_limitations: Not assessed for this area at the case cutoff; this is a gap, not a finding of no impact.
Historical validation case HVC-001, assessed at cutoff 2024-01-15T00:00:00Z. Later knowledge is deliberately excluded.
The duration of the diversion, its eventual freight-rate peak and its effect on Thailand trade volumes were all unknown at this cutoff and must not inform the assessment.

events.title: Francis Scott Key Bridge collapse suspends vessel traffic at the Port of Baltimore

lane_relevance.basis: lane includes country US; lane includes node NODE-USBAL

transmission_chain.operational_change: Vessel traffic into and out of the Port of Baltimore was suspended after the bridge collapse blocked the channel.
transmission_chain.logistics_mechanism: A closed channel removes a port from the service rotation and forces cargo to alternative United States East Coast gateways.
transmission_chain.observable_indicator: Port and coastguard notices, and port call counts for Baltimore and neighbouring East Coast ports.
transmission_chain.outcome: Observed suspension of Baltimore vessel traffic. No Thailand lane effect is established, because no evidence links Thailand ocean cargo to this port.

impact_assessments.geographic_scope: North America East and Gulf Coast, United States
known_limitations: No evidence links Thailand ocean cargo to the Port of Baltimore. The correct answer is insufficient evidence, not no impact: the platform has not assessed and disproved a Thailand effect, it has found no basis to assess one.

Historical validation case HVC-003, assessed at cutoff 2024-04-05T00:00:00Z. Later knowledge is deliberately excluded.

The channel reopening timetable and the eventual cargo diversion pattern were unknown at this cutoff. This case exists to check that a large, well-reported event with no established Thailand link produces 'insufficient evidence' rather than an invented transmission chain.

events.title: Elevated vessel waiting times reported at Singapore container terminals

lane_relevance.basis: lane includes country SG; lane includes node NODE-SGSIN; lane transits chokepoint CHK-SINGAPORE

thailand_relevance_basis: Singapore is the principal transshipment hub for Thailand long-haul ocean services, so waiting time at Singapore transmits to Thailand cargo through the transshipment leg.
The transmission is through transshipment connection times, not through any direct Thailand port condition.

transmission_chain.operational_change: Vessel waiting times before berthing at Singapore container terminals rose above their normal range.
transmission_chain.logistics_mechanism: Longer waiting before berthing delays discharge and reloading of transshipment boxes, which extends connection times for cargo relayed through the hub.
transmission_chain.observable_indicator: Port authority operational notices reporting waiting time, and berth occupancy measures.
transmission_chain.outcome: Observed capacity pressure at the hub, with potential service effects on Thailand cargo transshipped through it.

known_limitations: Not assessed for this area at the case cutoff; this is a gap, not a finding of no impact.
Not assessed for this area at the case cutoff; this is a gap, not a finding of no impact.
Not assessed for this area at the case cutoff; this is a gap, not a finding of no impact.
Not assessed for this area at the case cutoff; this is a gap, not a finding of no impact.
Not assessed for this area at the case cutoff; this is a gap, not a finding of no impact.
Not assessed for this area at the case cutoff; this is a gap, not a finding of no impact.

transmission_mechanism: Elevated waiting time before berthing reduces effective berth throughput at the hub.
known_limitations: Supported by an operational-condition notice from the port authority. Port call counts alone would not have supported this conclusion: throughput measures how much moved, not how long anything waited.

transmission_mechanism: Delayed discharge and reloading at the hub extends transshipment connection times for relayed Thailand cargo.
known_limitations: No transshipment connection-time series is qualified in this registry, so the Thailand-side effect is potential rather than measured.
Not assessed for this area at the case cutoff; this is a gap, not a finding of no impact.
Historical validation case HVC-004, assessed at cutoff 2024-06-10T00:00:00Z. Later knowledge is deliberately excluded.

How long the elevated waiting times persisted, and whether they spread to neighbouring hubs, was unknown at this cutoff. This case exists to check that a congestion conclusion requires an operational-condition source and is never derived from throughput alone.

clustering.title_normalized: elevated vessel waiting times reported at singapore container terminals

events.title: Pasir Panjang oil spill response with port operations reported unaffected
events.operator_or_entity: Maritime and Port Authority of Singapore

lane_relevance.basis: lane includes country SG; lane includes node NODE-SGSIN; lane transits chokepoint CHK-SINGAPORE

thailand_relevance_basis: Singapore is Thailand's principal transshipment hub, so a port event there is assessed for a Thailand transshipment effect even when none is found.

transmission_chain.operational_change: An oil spill response was mounted near Pasir Panjang Terminal.
transmission_chain.logistics_mechanism: A spill response can restrict navigation or berthing, which would delay vessel calls and transshipment connections.
transmission_chain.observable_indicator: Port authority notices stating whether navigation and berthing were restricted.
transmission_chain.outcome: The operator stated that navigational traffic and berthing operations were not affected, so no material logistics impact was detected.

known_limitations: The finding rests on the operating authority's own statement at the time of the notice and covers logistics operations only. Environmental and recreational effects are outside this conclusion.
The finding rests on the operating authority's own statement at the time of the notice and covers logistics operations only. Environmental and recreational effects are outside this conclusion.

Historical validation case HVC-007, assessed at cutoff 2024-06-20T00:00:00Z. Later knowledge is deliberately excluded.
This case exists to check the one legitimate use of 'no material impact detected': an actual assessment against explicit negative operational evidence, never an unassessed area.
events.closure_basis: The operating authority stated that navigation and berthing were unaffected, and no subsequent notice reported an operational restriction.
